title: Geniostoma waialealae
taxonomy: Geniostoma waialealae is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Hawaii, and its classification is as follows: Genus - Geniostoma, Family - Rubiaceae. The plant is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the madder or coffee family. Geniostoma waialealae is considered an endangered species, primarily due to habitat loss and degradation. The plant's classification is also reflective of its evolutionary relationships with other species within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Geniostoma waialealae has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties in Hawaiian and Polynesian cultures. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues.
